name: Rallybio Corp
business: Rallybio Corp is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing therapies for severe and rare diseases, with lead program RLYB116, a complement component 5 inhibitor, and RLYB332, a long-acting matriptase-2 antibody for iron overload disorders.

exposures.sentence: Our lead program, RLYB116, is a differentiated complement component 5 ( C5 ) inhibitor with the potential to treat diseases of complement dysregulation.
In addition, RLYB332, a long-acting matriptase-2 ("MTP-2") antibody for the treatment of diseases of iron overload, is currently in preclinical development.

catalysts.event: Completion of confirmatory Phase 1 clinical trial for RLYB116
catalysts.sentence: In 2025, we completed the confirmatory Phase 1 clinical trial evaluating the pharmacokinetic ("PK") / pharmacodynamic ("PD") properties of RLYB116.

catalysts.event: Reporting of confirmatory Phase 1 trial data for RLYB116
catalysts.sentence: We completed a confirmatory PK and PD study of RLYB116 in healthy volunteers in 2025 and reported data in the first quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Potential receipt of $5.0 million milestone payment from Recursion for Phase 1 study initiation of ENPP1 inhibitor
catalysts.sentence: We are eligible to receive a $5.0 million milestone payment in connection with the initiation of dosing in a Phase 1 clinical study, as defined in the ENPP1 Purchase Agreement and low single-digit royalties on all future net sales by Recursion of products comprising or incorporating certain compounds developed by REV-I.
We anticipate that the proposed merger with Candid will be completed in 2026.

catalysts.event: Disposition of Legacy Assets within one year after Closing of merger
catalysts.sentence: For a period of one year after the Closing Date, Rallybio will use commercially reasonable efforts to effect the disposition of the Legacy Assets.

tamClaims.claim: Over 25,000 annual cases of immune platelet transfusion refractoriness in the US and other major markets
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that there are more than 25,000 cases annually in the United States and other major markets.

tamClaims.claim: 130,000 people in the US and 120,000 in Europe with antiphospholipid syndrome
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that there are 130,000 people in the US and 120,000 in Europe who suffer from APS.
